shortcut: U short = English 'uh' (bus). UU long = rounded 'ee' (muur). OU/AU = English 'ow' (now).
explanation: The Dutch 'u' takes two forms — short and long — and they sound completely different. The diphthong 'ou' (and its identical twin 'au') is a third, separate sound. Mixing them up is the most common mistake at this level.

patterns.rule: Korte U — short, like English 'uh' in 'but'
examples: ↳ Spelling cue: a single 'u' followed by a consonant (closed syllable) → SHORT.
↳ Belgian phonetic: the short 'u' is roughly halfway between English 'u' in 'put' and 'u' in 'cut'.

patterns.rule: Lange UU — long, rounded vowel like German ü or French 'u'
examples: ik huur [huur] — I rent (verb form of huren)
↳ How to make it: shape your lips to say 'oo', then say 'ee' WITHOUT moving your lips. That's UU.
↳ Spelling cue: 'uu' (double) OR a single 'u' in an open syllable (mu-ren) → LONG.
↳ Same vowel: u-ren and uur sound IDENTICAL — only the spelling adapts.

patterns.rule: Tweeklank OU — diphthong, like English 'ow' in 'now'
examples: trouw [trow] — loyal / faithful · de trouw — loyalty
↳ AU and OU sound IDENTICAL in Dutch — both pronounced [ow]. Spelling is etymological, not phonetic.
↳ Examples of AU: blauw [blow] — blue · pauze [POW-zuh] — pause · auto [OW-toh] — car
bus       vs buur       vs bouw     ← these all look similar but sound different

memory: 🧠 Three sounds: U = quick 'uh' (bus, zus, nummer). UU = long rounded 'ee' (muur, duur, huur — lips like 'oo', say 'ee'). OU/AU = English 'ow' (mevrouw, jouw, koud). The OU and AU spellings make the SAME sound — never different.

examples.en: The rent is expensive. (two long UUs)

examples.nl: Mijn zus zit in de bus.
examples.en: My sister is on the bus. (three short Us)
Madam, may I have your number? (OU + OU + short U)

examples.nl: Het is koud op de bus.
examples.en: It is cold on the bus. (OU + short U)

examples.nl: De muur van het huis is blauw.
examples.en: The wall of the house is blue. (UU + 'ui' + AU)

practice.nl: Hoe spreek je 'bus' uit — kort of lang?
practice.en: How do you pronounce 'bus' — short or long?

practice.nl: Kort. [bus] — één u in een gesloten lettergreep.
practice.en: Short. [bus] — one u in a closed syllable.

practice.nl: Lang. [muur] — dubbele uu, lippen rond, tong vooraan.
practice.en: Long. [muur] — double uu, lips round, tongue forward.

practice.nl: Nee — exact hetzelfde. Blauw en mevrouw rijmen.
practice.en: No — exactly the same. Blauw and mevrouw rhyme.

practice.nl: [kowt] — net als de Engelse 'cow' met een -t.
